title: licania sclerophylla var. scabra
intro: Licania sclerophylla var. scabra, also known as the rough-leafed licania, is a plant species native to tropical regions, characterized by its thick, leathery leaves and small, white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of various compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Licania sclerophylla var. scabra include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the immune system. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
